title: Container fail with "Port(s) already bound: 80: Permission denied"
body:
### Issue Description

I have a Docker `compose.yml` that I ported to Podman `kube.yml` but Podman fails with

```
Port(s) already bound: 80: Permission denied
```

This should not be issue of rootless trying to use privileged port because I have configured

```
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start=80
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create `kube.yml` as

   ```yaml
   apiVersion: v1
   kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
   metadata:
     name: keycloak-postgres-pvc
   spec:
     storageClassName: "" # Empty string must be explicitly set otherwise default StorageClass will be set
     volumeName: keycloak-postgres-pv
   ---
   apiVersion: v1
   kind: Pod
   metadata:
     name: keycloak
     labels:
       app: keycloak
   spec:
     containers:
       - name: keycloak
         image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.2
         args:
           - "start-dev"
         env:
           - name: KC_HTTP_ENABLED
             value: "true"
           - name: KC_HTTP_PORT
             value: 80
           - name: KC_HEALTH_ENABLED
             value: "true"
           - name: KEYCLOAK_ADMIN
             value: "admin"
           - name: KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD
             value: "123"
           - name: KC_DB
             value: "postgres"
           - name: KC_DB_URL_HOST
             value: "postgres"
           - name: KC_DB_URL_DATABASE
             value: "keycloak"
           - name: KC_DB_USERNAME
             value: "admin"
           - name: KC_DB_PASSWORD
             value: "123"
   
       - name: postgres
         image: docker.io/library/postgres:18.0-alpine3.22
         env:
           - name: POSTGRES_DB
             value: "keycloak"
           - name: POSTGRES_USER
             value: "admin"
           - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
             value: "123"
         volumeMounts:
           - mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql
             name: keycloak-postgres-data
     volumes:
       - name: keycloak-postgres-data
         persistentVolumeClaim:
           claimName: keycloak-postgres-pvc
   ```
  
2. Run

   ```bash
   podman kube play --replace kube.yml
   ```


### Describe the results you received

The official Keycloak container fails due "Port(s) already bound: 80: Permission denied".

```
keycloak-keycloak   | Updating the configuration and installing your custom providers, if any. Please wait.
keycloak-keycloak   | 2025-11-11 14:42:45,314 INFO  [io.qua.dep.QuarkusAugmentor] (main) Quarkus augmentation completed in 15670ms
keycloak-postgres   | 
keycloak-postgres   | PostgreSQL Database directory appears to contain a database; Skipping initialization
keycloak-postgres   | 
keycloak-postgres   | �2025-11-11 14:42:27.245 UTC [1] LOG:  starting PostgreSQL 18.0 on x86_64-pc-linux-musl, compiled by gcc (Alpine 14.2.0) 14.2.0, 64-bit
keycloak-postgres   | 2025-11-11 14:42:27.245 UTC [1] LOG:  listening on IPv4 address "0.0.0.0", port 5432
keycloak-postgres   | 2025-11-11 14:42:27.245 UTC [1] LOG:  listening on IPv6 address "::", port 5432

keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:50,475 INFO  [org.infinispan.CONTAINER] (ForkJoinPool.commonPool-worker-1) ISPN000556: Starting user marshaller 'org.infinispan.jboss.marshalling.core.JBossUserMarshaller'
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:51,363 INFO  [org.keycloak.connections.infinispan.DefaultInfinispanConnectionProviderFactory] (main) Node name: node_103967, Site name: null
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:51,370 INFO  [org.keycloak.broker.provider.AbstractIdentityProviderMapper] (main) Registering class org.keycloak.broker.provider.mappersync.ConfigSyncEventListener
keycloak-keycloak   | 2025-11-11 14:42:52,686 INFO  [com.arjuna.ats.jbossatx] (main) ARJUNA032014: Stopping transaction recovery manager
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:52,726 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Failed to start server in (development) mode
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:52,726 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Unable to start HTTP server
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:52,727 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: io.quarkus.runtime.QuarkusBindException: Port(s) already bound: 80: Permission denied
keycloak-keycloak   | �2025-11-11 14:42:52,727 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Port(s) already bound: 80: Permission denied
keycloak-keycloak   | 2025-11-11 14:42:52,727 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) For more details run the same command passing the '--verbose' option. Also you can use '--help' to see the details about the usage of the particular command.
```

### Describe the results you expected

The official Keycloak container starts without problem. For comparison,

1. Create `compose.yml` as

   ```yaml
   services:
     keycloak:
       image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.2
       environment:
         KC_HTTP_ENABLED: true
         KC_HTTP_PORT: 80
         KC_HEALTH_ENABLED: true
         KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
         KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: 123
         KC_DB: postgres
         KC_DB_URL_HOST: keycloak-db
         KC_DB_URL_DATABASE: keycloak
         KC_DB_USERNAME: admin
         KC_DB_PASSWORD: 123
       command: ["start-dev"]
   
     keycloak-db:
       image: docker.io/library/postgres:18.0-alpine3.22
       environment:
         POSTGRES_USER: admin
         POSTGRES_PASSWORD: 123
         POSTGRES_DB: keycloak
       volumes:
         - keycloak-data:/var/lib/postgresql
   
   volumes:
     keycloak-data:
   ```

2. Run

   ```bash
   docker compose up
   ```

output in the logs

```
keycloak-db-1  | 
keycloak-db-1  | PostgreSQL Database directory appears to contain a database; Skipping initialization
keycloak-db-1  |

keycloak-1     | 2025-11-11 14:40:52,087 INFO  [org.keycloak.broker.provider.AbstractIdentityProviderMapper] (main) Registering class org.keycloak.broker.provider.mappersync.ConfigSyncEventListener
keycloak-1     | 2025-11-11 14:40:53,072 INFO  [io.quarkus] (main) Keycloak 25.0.2 on JVM (powered by Quarkus 3.8.5) started in 5.959s. Listening on: http://0.0.0.0:80. Management interface listening on http://0.0.0.0:9000.
keycloak-1     | 2025-11-11 14:40:53,072 INFO  [io.quarkus] (main) Profile dev activated. 
keycloak-1     | 2025-11-11 14:40:53,073 INFO  [io.quarkus] (main) Installed features: [agroal, cdi, hibernate-orm, jdbc-postgresql, keycloak, logging-gelf, narayana-jta, reactive-routes, resteasy-reactive, resteasy-reactive-jackson, smallrye-context-propagation, smallrye-health, vertx]
keycloak-1     | 2025-11-11 14:40:53,077 WARN  [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.KeycloakMain] (main) Running the server in development mode. DO NOT use this configuration in production.
```

comments.body:
Explained in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/13375#discussioncomment-6528096

Sorry for the noise.
